method: per-event independent rank shuffle of second-half events; same pipeline
interpretation: Pipeline has a structural null floor due to selection bias in assign_events_to_templates: even on rank-shuffled second-half data, the pipeline returns template_spearman ~0.75 and endpoint_position_recall ~0.67 because random events still get assigned to nearest cluster, and the mean rank of selected events drifts toward that cluster's template. The cohort medians sit ~0.17 above the null floor on these two metrics. swap_class_concordant has the cleanest signal: real 0.686 vs null 0.360 = +33pp. Calibration based on ONE subject (epilepsiae_548); per-subject null floors vary with n_valid_channels and n_events. Order-of-magnitude calibration only — tier rebalancing requires per-subject nulls in a future iteration.
scope_caveat: This calibration covers the structural pipeline null. It does NOT address (a) week-month timescale H_drift, (b) channel-selection circularity (lagPat is already high-HI selected), (c) per-subject n_valid heterogeneity.
user_review_2026_05_10: Per-subject null floor varies with n_valid / n_events. Single-subject null cannot support cohort-wide calibrated gain. Use only as supportive caveat. Paper-level tier rebalancing requires per-subject null or stratified null.
